invites.description:
PENDING invites only: consumed ones live on as the user's
provenance, expired ones are dead weight the UI need not show.

AnalyzerConfigRequest.description:
PUT /v1/analyzers/{family}: set a family's full config. `enabled`
toggles the opt-out flag; `params_hex` is an even-length hex string
(an odd or non-hex value is a 400), or null to clear.

AnalyzerInfo.description:
One family's enable state and opaque params. `params_hex` is the
analyzer-owned params (D60: the analyzer defines the encoding),
lowercase hex; null when unset.

AnalyzersResponse.description:
The shipped analyzer families and their per-family config — the same
data the CLI's `analyzer list` prints (owner-only).

error.description:
Human-readable failure description — diagnostic detail for CLI
and log consumers. The web UI presents `code`, not this.

BlobDetail.description:
The blob inspector: identity, digests, provenance, the one-hop
recipe-DAG neighborhood, satisfied claims, and pinning views. The
listing row's degree counts materialize here as the actual lists
(claims capped at 100 — `claims_total` is uncapped).

claims.description: Capped at 100 rows; `claims_total` is the uncapped count.

pins.description:
Views whose CURRENT snapshot references this blob (D33: the tag
is what pins).

provenance_via.description:
Source paths carried by recipe-CONNECTED blobs (D79 viral
provenance display): where these bytes actually arrived, for
derived/member blobs whose own `provenance` is empty. Capped
at 10.

roots.description:
Claimed blobs this blob is connected to through the recipe DAG
(D79): the display identity when `claims` is empty. Capped at
20; empty for a truly unattached blob.

routes_in.description: Recipes whose OUTPUT is this blob — ways to make it.

routes_out.description: Recipes consuming this blob as an input — things made from it.

sniff.description:
Magic-byte sniff of resident bytes ("zip archive", …) — a
display hint, never identity (D18: type lives in the edges).
Null when absent from disk or unrecognized.

BlobDigests.description:
Every digest the index holds for the blob's bytes: the blake3 key
plus recorded alias digests (D22). The declared CHD sha1 alias is
excluded — it attests decompressed content, not these bytes (D44).

verified_at.description:
Last full-hash store verification (ingest or scrub), unix
seconds; the index records no method, so none is claimed.

BlobRow.description:
One blob-index row with its graph degree — enough to answer "what
is this?" at listing granularity (`GET /v1/blobs/{hash}` expands
the edges).

routes_in.description: Non-poisoned recipes producing it (D25: `failed` never counts).

sources.description: source_file provenance rows naming this blob.

verified_at.description: Last full-hash store verification, unix seconds.

total.description: The FILTERED count; the offset/limit window slides under it.

ClaimRef.description:
One dat claim this blob satisfies, via identity links of any
evidence grade (current revisions only).

entry.description: Entry name within the source's current revision.

ClaimState.description:
Per-claim rollup state — the entry vocabulary plus the two
non-holding grades (`peer`, `probable`) that fold into `missing` at
entry granularity.

ClassBytes.description: One (namespace, residency) cell of the blob index.

bytes.description: SUM(size) with NULL sizes counted as 0; `sizeless` says how many.

sizeless.description: Blobs in this cell with no recorded size.

ClonelistResponse.description:
A linked retool clonelist's receipt (D57): the stored blob and the
mapping counts.

hash.description: blake3 of the stored clonelist blob, lowercase hex.

terms.description: searchTerm → group mappings usable by selection.

Counts.description: The D39 entry-audit rollup, bucketed into the 4-state vocabulary.

DatBytes.description:
Marker schema for the dat-import upload body: the raw dat file
bytes, not JSON (and not multipart — one file IS the request).

DatDiffResponse.description: The diff of a source's two newest revisions (previous → current, D38).

added.description: Entry names present only in the new revision.

rehashed.description: Matched entries whose claim content changed.

removed.description: Entry names present only in the old revision.

DatFetchRequest.description: Fetch a dat over HTTP and import it (Redump auto-fetch, D16).

provider.description:
Provider override; `redump/...` defaults to "Redump", else the
dat header decides.

system.description: System override; the dat header decides when absent.

DatFetchResponse.description:
A fetch receipt: the resolved URL that was fetched, plus the import
outcome (the artifact went through the normal import path — D15).

import.description: The import receipt for the fetched dat.

url.description: The URL actually fetched (a `redump/...` source resolves here).

DatImportResponse.description:
One import's receipt — the same fields `datboi dat import --json`
prints, plus the resolved source identity (the web caller never saw
the dat header, so it can't derive provider/system itself).

dat_blob.description: blake3 of the dat blob now in the CAS, lowercase hex.

demoted_revisions.description: Revisions demoted to header-only by this import (D38).

provider.description:
Resolved provider (override, else derived from the dat header —
homepage when it names an org, else author).

source_id.description: The dat source this landed in — the `/v1/systems` id.

system.description: Resolved system (override, else the dat header's name).

DatImportedItem.description:
One dat an ingest job imported (the unified drop surface: content
classifies each file as dat / zipped dat / ROM — names never
decide).

path.description: The client's original name for the file.

provider.description:
Resolved provider/system — the `/v1/systems` identity the dat
landed in (the caller never saw the dat header).

DatNameChange.description:
An entry name that changed across revisions: `from` → `to`. For
`renamed` the content is identical and only the name moved; for
`rehashed` the claim content changed (and `from == to` when only the
content, not the name, differs).

Definition.description: ViewDef summary: dat ref, 1G1R mode, profile, image params (D62).

Endpoints.description: Relative serve endpoints; DAV is loopback-only in M5 (D68).

total.description: The FILTERED count; the offset/limit window slides under it.

EntryDetail.description:
Entry detail is keyed by NAME (`UNIQUE(revision_id, name)` within a
revision; claim names are not unique — MAME sets repeat them).

size.description:
Total required bytes — null unless every required claim
declares a size.

wanted_hash.description:
Best hash of the single required claim, full hex — null when
the entry wants zero or several ROMs (the detail endpoint has
them all).

EntryState.description:
The UI's 4-state per-entry vocabulary, derived from the D39 rollup
(web spec §7). `nodump` entries are excluded from completeness math
client-side.

ErrorCode.description:
Machine-readable failure category (D77). A CLOSED union: the web UI
maps every variant to a translated message through an exhaustive
switch, so adding a variant here fails the web typecheck until that
message exists — errors are translatable by construction, never by
passing English prose through.

The HTTP status derives FROM the code ([`ErrorCode::http_status`]),
so a handler cannot pair a code with a contradicting status.

EvictBlocked.description:
One candidate the plan cannot drop right now, with the D25/D27 reasons
spelled out (a residency planner that says "0 evicted" without saying
why loses trust).

EvictPlan.description: The dry-run eviction plan: what would drop, and what is held back.

blocked.description: Candidates held back, each with its reasons.

evictable.description: Blobs that would be evicted at the current target.

EvictRequest.description:
Reclaim resident bytes by evicting recipe-covered literals (D25/D72).
`dry_run` reports the plan without deleting (the preview surface);
otherwise the drop runs under the D72 singleton guard as a background
Gc job — a 202 with a job id, or a 503 if the guard is already held.

dry_run.description: Report the plan without deleting anything. Defaults to false.

license.description:
License (replay) covered routes first, so routes that have not
yet replayed on this host become evictable too. Defaults to false.

target_bytes.description: Evict until at most this many resident bytes remain.

hash.description: blake3 hex of the file content.

GcApplyRequest.description:
Apply the reviewed set (D73: the ONE human-triggered destructive
action). Absent `hashes` = every reviewable, non-kept candidate.

skipped.description:
Requested but refused at delete-time re-verification (rooted
since review, kept, or not aged) — never an error, always
visible.

GcConfig.description:
The eviction/GC policy: high/low watermarks (canonical `off` / `NN%`
/ absolute-bytes strings) and the orphan grace window in seconds.

GcConfigRequest.description:
PUT /v1/gc/config: set any subset of the policy. Each omitted field
is left unchanged; a malformed watermark or negative grace is a 400.

GcKeepRequest.description: Set or clear a keep-mark ("this is not junk").

HashRef.description: A blob reference inside a route edge.

name.description:
The recipe_output member name (or recipe_input role), when
recorded.

ImageBytes.description:
Marker schema for the minted-image download body: raw octets, not
JSON.

ImageParams.description: FAT32 image parameters from the ViewDef (D62).

ImageStatus.description:
D62 mint status: the `image/<name>` tag, when present, is a minted
FAT32 image for this view.

oneOf.description:
Image size; omitted when unminted, null when the blob row has
no recorded size (outer Option/inner Nullable = absent/null).

hash.description: blake3 hex of the minted image; omitted when unminted.

IngestErrorItem.description: A file the pipeline could not process.

path.description: The client's original name for the file.

IngestMemberSkipItem.description:
An archive member the pipeline refused (zip64, encrypted,
unsupported method, central-directory lies, …).

path.description: The client's original name for the container.

IngestReportBody.description:
The ingest pipeline's report (datboi-ingest `IngestReport`), as
wire JSON. Same counters the CLI prints.

dats_imported.description:
Files the job classified (by content) as dats — loose or the
sole member of a zip — and imported instead of ingesting. Its
own lane: pipeline counters above stay pure, a dat import is
not a `files_scanned`.

files_unchanged.description: Rescan-cache hits: same path, mtime, and size as last time.

members_claimed.description: Zip members claimed in place (the container stays the literal).

skipper_skipped_large.description:
Files over the skipper sniff cap — stored, but not
detector-checked.

IngestRequest.description: Start an ingest job over staged uploads.

job.description: Poll `GET /v1/jobs/{id}` for progress and the report.

InviteAcceptRequest.description:
Invite acceptance: consumes the invite atomically, creates the user
with the invite's role, and starts a session.

token.description: The invite token exactly as minted (the URL fragment).

InviteMintRequest.description:
Invite mint parameters. An unknown `role` variant is the
extractor's typed 400 (`ApiJson`), so the field is a real [`Role`].

expires_days.description:
1-365; defaults to 7 (D68) — an effectively-eternal invite is a
standing credential, which is what invites exist to avoid.

InviteMintResponse.description:
The mint answer: a fragment URL (`/invite#<token>`) so the token
never appears in server logs or Referer headers.

InviteRow.description:
A pending invite. Only `blake3(token)` is stored — the token itself
appeared exactly once, in the mint response's fragment URL.

created_by.description: Minting username; null for loopback/CLI mints (no user row).

token_hash.description: blake3 of the token string, lowercase hex — the revocation key.

Job.description:
One row of the job registry (running + D74 ledger history — the
activity page render, D82).

progress.description:
0–100, byte-weighted at FILE granularity (the pipeline reports
no intra-file progress); clamped to 99 while running so only a
finished job reads 100.

started_at.description:
Unix seconds. On the LIST row, not just the detail, because the
activity page renders relative timestamps for every row and
must not need N+1 detail fetches to do it (D82).

current.description:
Original name of the file being processed; omitted between
files and once finished.

error.description:
Infrastructure failure only; per-file refusals live in the
report.

matched.description:
Entries this job's content NEWLY satisfied (the shelf lights).
Capped at 200 rows; `matched_total` is the uncapped count.

report.description:
Cumulative across completed files — grows while running, final
when done.

oneOf.description:
The savings summary of a finished Sync job (D101); absent for
every other kind and while the sync is still running. `default`
so pre-D101 ledger rows still hydrate.

JobDetail.description:
GET /v1/jobs/{id}: the row plus counters and the (growing, then
final) report. File paths in the report are the CLIENT's original
names — staging paths never leak.

JobKind.description:
What a job is doing. Under D96 the expensive view verbs graduated to
background jobs on the serve surface (eval, mint); evict/sweep follow.

JobStartResponse.description:
A started background job (D96 view verbs: eval, mint; maintenance
verbs: scrub). Poll `GET /v1/jobs/{id}` for progress; the finished
job's report carries the outcome, a failed one carries `error`.

LastScrub.description: The scrub-run ledger readout (D74 rows, kind scrub).

name.description: The run's ledger name (carries the sample %).

LoginRequest.description:
Login credentials. Missing fields are the extractor's typed 400
(datboi-server's `ApiJson` keeps every body-parse failure in the
D69 error shape), so plain `String` IS the required-field contract.

MameMode.description: MAME set rendering mode (D31 deferred set).

MatchedEntry.description:
One dat entry an ingest job newly satisfied — the user-vocabulary
half of the report (games, not blob counts).

OkResponse.description: The `{"ok": true}` acknowledgement mutations answer with.

OneGOneRMode.description:
1G1R selection mode (D57): `strict` is a pure function of
(dat, preferences); `held_first` prefers what you actually have.

OrphanItem.description:
One reviewable orphan (D73): unreferenced past the grace window,
still unrooted as of the last sweep. The mark is REVIEW state —
apply re-verifies reachability at delete time.

kept.description: Operator keep-mark: excluded from apply until unkept.

marked_at.description: Unix seconds of first-observed-unreferenced (the grace clock).

sources.description:
Ingest paths that ever carried these bytes — the context a
reviewer needs to say junk-or-keep.

reclaimable_bytes.description: Bytes across non-kept candidates (what apply-all would free).

P2pStatusResponse.description:
The daemon's p2p plane (D97: opt-in via `--p2p`). `endpoint_id` is
the iroh EndpointId peers dial — the "share this as your peer id"
string the daemon logs at startup, surfaced so the web can show it.

enabled.description: Whether the seedbox is live (started with `--p2p` AND bound).

endpoint_id.description: Our iroh EndpointId (z-base-32); null when p2p is off.

P2pSyncRequest.description:
Reconcile with a peer and fetch the diff (D100 flow, D101 surface):
plans reconcile via the rateless-IBLT sketch, missing pieces fetch as
verified blobs, explicit wants rebuild locally. Long-running
(network), so it answers a `JobStartResponse`; the finished Sync
job's `sync` detail carries the savings summary.

peer.description: The peer's iroh EndpointId (z-base-32, as their daemon logs it).

wants.description:
Container hashes to rebuild locally (blake3, 64 hex chars).
Empty or absent = mirror mode (D100): ground everything the
peer's plans describe — leaves fetched, nothing materialized.

ProvenanceRow.description: One rescan-cache row: a filesystem path that hashed to this blob.

ingested_at.description: When the path was last scanned/ingested, unix seconds.

ProvenanceViaRow.description:
One viral-provenance row (D79): a source path recorded on a
recipe-connected blob, shown on blobs that have none of their own.

ingested_at.description: When the path was last scanned/ingested, unix seconds.

via.description: The connected blob (blake3 hex) actually carrying the path.

Quarantine.description: D49 rule 3: components whose seek path produced bad bytes.

ResidencyState.description:
Blob residency (D21): bytes on disk, evicted-but-rebuildable, or
known-absent.

Role.description: User roles (D30/D68): owners see everything, friends see grants.

RomBytes.description:
Marker schema for the ingest upload body: the raw file bytes,
streamed to staging — same one-file-IS-the-request shape as dats.

RomClaim.description:
One rom claim with its rollup state and, when a local blob
satisfies it, the resolved blob + rebuild routes + pin list.

oneOf.description:
The satisfying local blob (strong-basis links only, smallest
hash wins) — omitted entirely when nothing satisfies the claim.

hashes.description:
Declared digests, lowercase hex; only the algorithms the dat
actually carries appear.

pins.description:
Views whose CURRENT snapshots reference the resolved blob
(D33: the tag is what pins); omitted when `blob` is.

routes.description: Rebuild routes for the resolved blob; omitted when `blob` is.

RomHashes.description:
Declared claim digests (lowercase hex); absent algorithms are
omitted, not null.

RootRef.description: A claimed root reachable from a blob through recipe edges (D79).

entry.description: Best claim name on the root.

hash.description: The root blob (blake3 hex) — links into its own inspector.

RootRelation.description:
How the inspected blob relates to a claimed root, following
DIRECTED recipe edges (input → output = "helps make").

RouteEdge.description:
One recipe edge of the DAG neighborhood, navigable — every
[`HashRef`] resolves at `GET /v1/blobs/{hash}`. Poisoned (`failed`)
recipes never surface (D25 terminal).

op.description: The op as recorded: builtin `name@major`, wasm `<hex>#export`.

RouteInfo.description:
One human-readable rebuild route: `verb ← sources` plus the
source-availability dot.

RouteVerify.description:
Recipe verification grade (D25); `failed` recipes are poisoned and
never surface as routes.

ScrubRequest.description:
Trigger a corpus scrub over serve — the same walk `datboi scrub`
runs (verify bytes against names, back-fill aliases + `verified_at`,
pack integrity, optional recipe rehabilitation). Long-running, so it
answers a `JobStartResponse`; the finished Scrub job's notes carry
the checked/refreshed/corrupt/missing summary.

rehabilitate.description:
Re-execute poisoned (Failed) recipes; a verified re-replay clears
the poison. Defaults to false.

sample_pct.description:
Percentage of loose blobs to check (deterministic sample by hash
prefix); packs are always scrubbed whole when this is non-zero.
Defaults to 100 (a full pass), matching the CLI.

SessionResponse.description:
A fresh login/acceptance answer: whoami-shaped plus the session
expiry. The session token itself rides the `Set-Cookie` header
(HttpOnly), never the body.

SnapshotResponse.description:
The report of one minted state snapshot — the same numbers `datboi
snapshot` prints. Content-dedup means an unchanged shard costs no new
batch blob, so `new_batch_blobs` is the actual write amplification.

alias_rows.description: Alias rows batched across the shard fanout.

hash.description: The minted snapshot object's blake3 (64 hex chars).

new_batch_blobs.description: Batch blobs newly written this mint (unchanged shards dedup away).

SourceBytes.description:
Data-namespace bytes attributed to one dat source via identity
links (identity_blob → rom_claim → entry → the source's CURRENT
dat_revision). A blob claimed by several sources counts in EACH
row — the column does not sum to the store. Blobs with no identity
link at all fold into the `(unattributed)` row.

StorageBreakdown.description:
Where the bytes live: the debug-grade aggregate behind the storage
screen — per-(namespace, residency) accounting, dat-source
attribution, and the largest blobs.

StorageResponse.description:
Storage stats from the blob index — the same truth `datboi status`
reports, without an NFS metadata walk.

last_scrub.description:
Newest finished scrub from the D74 ledger; null before the
first recorded run.

literal_only_bytes.description:
Resident bytes with no non-failed rebuild route — the "can't
shrink yet" tax.

represented_bytes.description:
Resident + evicted-covered bytes (what the collection
represents).

SweepRequest.description:
Run one analyzer sweep round on demand — the manual equivalent of the
ambient refiner's per-family drain (D71). Long-running (a preflate
split is minutes), so it answers a `JobStartResponse`; the finished
Refine job's note carries the enqueued/analyzed/positive/negative
counts and the remaining queue depth.

analyzer.description:
Analyzer name (canonical or CLI alias): noop, chunk, preflate,
ecm, nds, narc.

limit.description:
Maximum items to analyze this round. Defaults to 10000 (the CLI
default).

SyncSummary.description:
What one sync moved, held back, and rebuilt (D97: the savings ARE
the result). All counters are u64 so the summary stays exact; the
headline percentage is derived client-side as
`100 * (1 - bytes_fetched / bytes_rebuilt)` — never baked into
prose server-side (D101).

bytes_fetched.description: Total bytes pulled off the wire (sketch + plans + pieces).

peer.description: The peer's iroh EndpointId this sync ran against.

pieces_already_held.description: Leaves the plans needed that we already held — the dedup win.

pieces_fetched.description: Grounding leaves fetched (the D91 pieces we lacked).

recipes_fetched.description: Recipe objects (plans) fetched and indexed from the peer.

System.description:
A "system" is a `dat_source` row. `id` is a cache.db surrogate —
stable while the cache lives, re-minted by `datboi recover`;
clients treat it as a handle, not an identity (the durable identity
is provider/system).

revision.description: Null when the source exists but nothing was imported yet.

total.description: Total entries in the current revision (counts sum to this).

views.description: Stored ViewDef names referencing this source.

upload.description:
Opaque staging token — spend it in `POST /v1/ingest`. Tokens
are in-memory: a daemon restart forgets them (the staged bytes
are swept with the rest of the store's tmp/).

job.description:
Poll `GET /v1/jobs/{id}`; done stamps `verified_at`, failed
carries the corruption evidence in `error`.

Via.description:
How a caller's identity arrived (whoami reports it). `loopback` is
the D68 implicit owner — a local shell already owns the daemon's
files.

ViewDefineRequest.description:
PUT /v1/views/{name}: define or replace a view (D96 view authoring;
the CLI's `view define`). The name is the path segment — this body
is its definition. `one_g_one_r` and `mame_mode` are mutually
exclusive (families vs sets answer different questions); setting both
is a 400. Omitting all selection fields defines an all-entries view.
Mirrors the read-side [`Definition`], but with omittable optionals
(request convention: `T` for required, `Option<T>` + skip for
optional — see [`wire`]).

oneOf.description: `Some` = the view also reifies as a FAT32 image (D62).
`Some` = render a MAME listxml source as merge-mode sets.
`Some` = 1G1R over clone families with these priorities.

profile.description:
Built-in constraint profile name (see `GET /v1/view-profiles`).
An unknown name is a 400.

template.description:
Layout template. Placeholders `{entry}` / `{name}`; path
separators inside expanded values are sanitized to `_`.

ViewDefineResponse.description:
PUT /v1/views/{name} result: the stored definition, echoed back so
the UI can render the view without a follow-up read.

image.description:
Mint status when the definition carries image params; null when
it does not.

ViewDetail.description:
GET /v1/views/{name}: the summary plus serve endpoints and mint
status.

ViewFilesPage.description:
A page of the view's CURRENT snapshot manifest — the friend browse
surface (web spec §4.3).

snapshot.description: The snapshot these rows came from (blake3 hex).

total.description: The FILTERED count; the offset/limit window slides under it.

ViewProfile.description:
One constraint profile. Mirrors `datboi-catalog`'s built-in `Profile`
(the CLI's `view profiles` renders the same three limits).

max_dir_entries.description:
Directories with more children than this are reported; null = no
limit enforced.

max_file_size.description:
Files larger than this don't fit the target at all; null = no
ceiling (e.g. exFAT targets).

ViewProfilesResponse.description:
GET /v1/view-profiles: the built-in constraint profiles a view
definition may name (the target-device limits an evaluation checks
against — docs/views.md). Static built-in data; the define UI offers
these as the `profile` choice.

ViewSummary.description:
One view in the listing: the union of tagged views (things being
served) and stored ViewDefs (defined but maybe never evaluated).
Row/byte/created stats come from the decoded snapshot manifest; a
missing or undecodable snapshot just omits them — the listing must
not die of one damaged view.

definition.description: Null for tagged views whose definition was deleted.

snapshot.description:
Current snapshot hash (blake3 hex); null when defined but never
evaluated.

WhoamiResponse.description:
Whoami is open by design: it answers `authenticated: false` instead
of 401 so the SPA can probe without special-casing errors.

username.description: Absent for loopback (no user row) and anonymous callers.

bearer_token.description: The same session token as `Authorization: Bearer` — for tools.

session_cookie.description: Browser session cookie (HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax), minted by login/invite acceptance.
session_cookie.name: datboi_session

info.description: The datboi daemon's JSON API (D69 contract). Loopback callers are implicitly the owner (D68); non-loopback callers authenticate with a session cookie or bearer token. The `/view/*`, `/snap/*`, and `/dav` byte-serving surfaces are outside this contract.
info.title: datboi /v1

post.summary:
Grant a friend a view. Grants on views that exist nowhere (no tag,
no definition) are refused as typos, not recorded as policy.

Mint an invite. The token appears exactly once, in the answered
fragment URL; the database stores only its blake3.

parameters.description: blake3 of the token, 64 hex chars
parameters.name: token_hash

delete.summary: Revoke a pending invite by its stored token hash.
Revoke every session a user holds.

get.summary: Users with grants and live-session counts, plus pending invites.
The shipped analyzer families and their per-family config.

400.description: Unknown family or malformed params hex

put.summary: Set one family's enable state and opaque params (idempotent).

200.description: User created, session started (session cookie set)

400.description: Missing field / bad username / short password

post.summary:
Accept an invite: consumes it atomically, creates the user with the
invite's role, starts a session (Set-Cookie on the response).

Log in. One uniform 401 for wrong password and unknown user
(unknown users still pay for an argon2 verify — timing).

Log out: deletes the presented session (cookie or bearer) and
clears the cookie either way.

get.summary:
Who am I? Open: answers `authenticated: false` instead of 401 so
the SPA can probe without special-casing errors.

parameters.description: Case-insensitive blake3-hex prefix; empty = no filter
parameters.name: q

parameters.description: Keep only blobs in this residency
parameters.name: residency

200.description: One page, hash-ordered; `total` counts the filtered set

get.summary: Page through the blob index — the inspector's search surface.

One blob, fully explained: digests, provenance, the one-hop recipe
DAG around it, the dat claims it satisfies, and the views pinning
it.

206.description: Requested range of the blob bytes

404.description: No such blob (or no bytes to serve)

get.summary:
Raw blob bytes by hash — the fetch half of the D84 BIOS-from-CAS
design (emulator cores name accepted system-file hashes; the Play
screen asks for each until one answers). Serves through the same
verified-range machinery as /view files (ETag = the hash, Range
honored). Owner-only like the rest of the blob surface: friends'
byte access stays view-scoped.

200.description: Blob is resident (replayed, or already was)

500.description: Replay failed (no usable route, or transform error)

post.summary:
Rematerialize an evicted/claimed blob by replaying its rebuild route
(D25/D27/D96): owner-only, synchronous, idempotent.

400.description: Not a blake3 hex hash / blob not on disk

post.summary:
Verify one blob right now (D80): re-hash the resident bytes,
stamp `verified_at` on match, fail the job with evidence on
mismatch. The second pipeline verb to graduate from CLI-only —
verification is the product's core promise, and "when was this
last checked?" is exactly when the user must be able to act.
Resident literals only: a rebuildable blob verifies by replay,
which stays CLI.

400.description: Bad source, fetch failure, or unparseable dat

post.summary:
Fetch a dat over HTTP and import it (Redump auto-fetch, D16/D96) —
the same operation as `datboi dat fetch`. The network request runs
off the pipeline writer; the fetched artifact goes through the normal
import path (D15).

parameters.description: Dat source provider override (default derives from the dat header: homepage when it names an org, else author)
parameters.name: provider

parameters.description: Dat source system override (default: the dat header's name)
parameters.name: system

200.description: Imported: a new revision of a new or existing source

413.description: Dat larger than the upload limit (plain-text body — the reject fires below the JSON layer)

post.summary:
Import a dat file — the same operation as `datboi dat import`. The
body is the raw dat bytes in any supported format (Logiqx,
ClrMamePro, RomCenter, MAME XML / software list); format detection
and the provider/system defaults come from the bytes themselves.

Link a retool clonelist to a source (D57/D96) — the same operation as
`datboi dat clonelist`. The body is the raw clonelist JSON.

400.description: Source has only one materialized revision

get.summary:
Diff a source's two newest revisions (previous → current, D38/D96) —
the same output as `datboi dat diff`. A pure read; an empty diff is a
200 with empty lists.

404.description: No such source or no current revision

get.summary:
Export a source's current revision as a Logiqx dat (dir2dat, D29/D96)
— the same bytes `datboi export dat` writes. A raw XML download.

503.description: GC guard busy (eviction in progress); retry

post.summary:
Reclaim resident bytes by evicting recipe-covered literals (D72/D96,
owner-only). `dry_run` answers the plan (200); a real run starts a
guarded background Gc job (202) or is refused if the guard is busy
(503).

get.summary: Read the eviction/GC policy (owner-only).

put.summary: Set any subset of the eviction/GC policy (owner-only).

400.description: Missing field / not a blake3 hash

post.summary:
Set or clear a keep-mark ("this is not junk") — authoritative
state, survives cache rebuilds, excludes the blob from apply.

get.summary:
Reviewable orphan candidates (D73): unreferenced data blobs past
the grace window, with ingest provenance and keep-marks. Marks are
review state — apply re-verifies at delete time.

503.description: GC guard busy (eviction in progress); retry

post.summary:
Apply the reviewed set — the one human-triggered destructive action
(D73). Every deletion re-verifies unreferenced + aged + unkept at
delete time under the D72 singleton guard; refusals count as
`skipped`, never errors.

400.description: Missing/empty uploads, or an unknown/expired token

post.summary:
Ingest staged uploads: spends the tokens all-or-nothing, answers a
job id immediately, and runs the pipeline in the background — the
same hash/claim/archive semantics as `datboi ingest`, one file at a
time. Custody over HTTP is always copy: the browser cannot move
your originals.

parameters.description: Client-relative file name (`/`-separated, no `..`); report entries wear this name
parameters.name: name

200.description: Staged; spend the token in POST /v1/ingest

400.description: Missing/bad name, empty body, or aborted/short body

507.description: Insufficient store headroom for the declared Content-Length

post.summary:
Stage one file for ingest: the body is the raw bytes, streamed to
the store's staging area (never buffered in memory — files run to
GBs). The answered token is spent in `POST /v1/ingest`; tokens and
staged bytes are ephemeral (daemon restart forgets them, the
staging sweep removes them).

200.description: Running + recently finished jobs, newest first

get.summary:
The in-memory job registry: running jobs plus recently finished
ones (the registry keeps a bounded tail; a daemon restart forgets
everything — durable job reports are a recorded open question).

404.description: No such job (or the registry forgot it)

get.summary: One job with counters and its (growing, then final) ingest report.

The daemon's p2p plane (D97/D101): whether the seedbox is live, and
the EndpointId to share with friends. Owner-only — the endpoint id
is the capability peers dial (D8), not public data.

400.description: Malformed peer id or want hash

503.description: P2p is disabled — start the daemon with --p2p (D101)

post.summary:
Reconcile with a peer and fetch the diff (D100/D101, owner-only):
dedup-aware transfer — plans reconcile via the sketch, only missing
pieces cross the wire, explicit wants rebuild locally (empty wants =
mirror mode). Long-running (network), so it starts a Sync job; the
finished job's `sync` detail is the savings summary.

Scrub the corpus (owner-only, D96): verify bytes against their names,
back-fill aliases + `verified_at`, check pack integrity, and
optionally rehabilitate poisoned recipes — the same walk `datboi
scrub` runs. Long-running, so it answers a job id.

Mint a state snapshot on demand (D75/D96, owner-only): the manual
trigger beside the daemon's dirty-triggered auto-cadence — the same
`datboi snapshot` mint. Synchronous; answers the mint report.

get.summary:
Storage stats from the blob index — `datboi status` without the
filesystem walk.

Where the bytes live: per-(namespace, residency) accounting,
dat-source attribution (a blob claimed by several sources counts in
each; unlinked blobs fold into `(unattributed)`), and the 50
largest data blobs.

post.summary:
Run one analyzer sweep round on demand (D71/D96, owner-only): the
manual equivalent of the ambient refiner's per-family drain. Long-
running, so it starts a background Refine job.

get.summary:
Imported dat sources with their audit rollups — the JSON render of
`datboi audit`.

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring over entry names; empty = no filter
parameters.name: q

parameters.description: Keep only entries in this state
parameters.name: state

200.description: One page; `total` counts the filtered set

get.summary:
Page through a system's entries with state filter and
case-insensitive substring search.

Entry detail by NAME (unique within a revision), with per-claim
hashes, resolved blobs, rebuild routes, and pin lists.

The built-in constraint profiles a definition may name (owner-only:
authoring support, same surface as define).

Views visible to the caller: owners see everything, friends see
exactly their grants (D68).

404.description: No such view (or not granted — indistinguishable)

get.summary:
One view with serve endpoints and image-mint status. Denial answers
exactly like a miss so probing learns nothing.

400.description: Bad definition (unknown profile, or both selection modes)

put.summary:
Define or replace a view by name (owner-only; idempotent upsert). The
name is the path; the body is the definition. 1G1R and MAME modes are
mutually exclusive (400 if both). An unknown profile is a 400.

post.summary:
Evaluate a view into a fresh immutable snapshot (owner-only). Long-
running, so it starts a background job (D96); poll `GET /v1/jobs/{id}`.

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring over full manifest paths
parameters.name: q

200.description: One page; `total` counts the filtered set

get.summary:
Flat page of the view's CURRENT snapshot manifest — the friend
browse surface.

206.description: Requested range of the image bytes

404.description: No such view, not granted, or no image minted

get.summary:
Download the minted FAT32 image (D62). Standard Range/ETag
semantics: strong content-hash ETag, `Accept-Ranges: bytes`, 206
partial responses, `Content-Disposition: attachment`.

post.summary:
Mint the view's FAT32 image from its current snapshot (owner-only,
D62). Long-running (materialize + mint), so it starts a background
job; the minted image downloads at `GET /v1/views/{name}/image`. A
never-evaluated view is a 400.
